code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1 Star (Very Negative):
    - Strong dissatisfaction or anger with safety risks/complete failure
    - Multiple product failures override initial positive experience
    - Explicit "don't buy" recommendations with evidence of defects
    - Negative updates take precedence over initial satisfaction

    2 Stars (Negative):
    - Core functionality compromised despite isolated positives
    - Regret expressed through phrases like "waste of money"
    - Product requires constant workarounds for basic functionality
    - Comparative inferiority to alternatives ("worse than X")

    3 Stars (Neutral / Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with contextual limitations
    - Conditional praise without recommendation ("good IF...")
    - Technical competence with notable compromises
    - Target audience restrictions without endorsement

    4 Stars (Positive):
    - Explicit star deduction phrases ("only reason not 5 stars...")
    - Single functional criticism impacting core utility (absorbency/sizing)
    - Requires break-in period/preprocessing for full functionality
    - Brief positive statements lacking enthusiasm ("works fine")
    - Resolved issues mentioned as lingering drawbacks

    5 Stars (Very Positive):
    - 3+ complementary aspects praised (functionality + design + durability)
    - Long-term validation (6+ months) with sustained performance
    - Non-functional criticism with enthusiastic core praise
    - "Worth every penny" declarations with demonstrated utility
    - Practical benefits significantly outweigh stated limitations
    - Explicit "still 5 stars" endorsements despite minor imperfections

    BOUNDARY CLARIFICATIONS:
    - Functional praise + 1 non-functional criticism →5
    - Functional praise + 1 functional criticism →4
    - "Would be 5 stars but..." →4 if functional, →5 if non-functional
    - Multi-purchase failures →1 regardless of initial experience
    - Temporary issues →4 if lingering mention, →5 if enthusiastically resolved
    - Inherent material properties ≠ functional flaws
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: "UPDATE: Second unit failed faster than first. Dangerous defects!"
<Reasoning>Multiple safety-related failures override any positives.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: "Does exactly what needed with deep pockets. No complaints."
<Reasoning>Brief functional praise without enthusiasm or drawbacks. Solid 4-star.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: "Perfect size and silent operation! Lid occasionally sticks but still 5 stars."
<Reasoning>Functional excellence outweighs minor non-functional quirk. Explicit 5-star endorsement.</Rating>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: "These towels need 2 washes for absorbency but work well after. Great design."
<Reasoning>Functional prerequisite mentioned as drawback despite resolution. Lingering criticism →4.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: "Love how this organizes my closet! Durable, stylish, and space-saving."
<Reasoning>Three complementary strengths praised without negatives. Clear 5-star.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: "Beautiful but leaks constantly. Can't recommend despite looks."
<Reasoning>Core failure overrides aesthetic appeal. Regret language →2.</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: "EDIT: After 1 year, still perfect! Minor noise doesn't affect results."
<Reasoning>Long-term validation with trivial drawback. Sustained excellence →5.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: "Good for light use. Handle feels cheap during heavy tasks."
<Reasoning>Contextual limitations with material compromise. Neutral →3.</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 9:
Review: "Makes great smoothies but overheats. Useless for daily use."
<Reasoning>Core functionality failure despite good results. Frustration →2.</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 10:
Review: "Cotton wrinkles but absorbs better than competitors. Worth every penny!"
<Reasoning>Material property outweighed by core functionality praise →5.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>
</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
Rating:
</Task>
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (30 total misclassified):
  gold 4 -> 5: 11
  gold 5 -> 4: 9
  gold 4 -> 3: 4
  gold 5 -> 3: 2
  gold 1 -> 2: 2

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "We own a huge amount of Lodge cast iron cook and bake ware plus a few odd pieces collected over the years. Although I've tried other brands in the past, Lodge remained the best. Now, I'd like to say this was equal or better than Lodge but it's not......"
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "These skull and bones are plenty big to be seen from a distance. You get two in the package and they measure 21"x18.5". You could use them in your home or office. Even though these are the pirate skull and bones, they are smiling and don't look scary..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [5, 3, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  3. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 2, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  4. Review: "This is a good, though not extra ordinary storage bin. It lacks exceptional build quality, exceptional attention to detail, or anything that's all that remarkable. It looks good, and the pattern is timeless, plus it keeps stuff together. You could do..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.33
  5. Review: "I really like these potholders. They look great and do a good job of keeping hot pots and pans and other dishes out of the oven off the table and counters. They had a bit of a plastic smell when they arrived but after I washed them (they held up fine..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "I bought one of those Pyrex sets a few years ago.  You know, the one that came with the blue lids and some of the containers were rectangular --large and small-- while others were round (like these).  Well, turns out that the ones I used the most wer..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "We are both tough on dish towels and use them constantly. Not only do we cook 90% of our meals at home but we also use fresh fruits and veggies with pretty much every meal and that means a lot of washing, drying and carrying things around...why carry..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "Very nice set of skillets - and what a lovely color.  Quite a bargain, and I was glad to find it as I provide my husband his "own" skillets.  He tends to be a "high heat" cook, and so I don't let him use my expensive skillets.  Was nice to find Farbe..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 4, 5] | Disagreement: 0.17
  2. Review: "Over the past two months or so I've gotten numerous sets of kitchen towels and the process was always the same. Try them. See the weren't absorbent. Wash them numerous times with fabric softener to make them somewhat absorbent and keep at least four ..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "The shower rod in my main bathroom is adjustable, so it has a bit of a bump where the two pieces.  My old shower hooks had a terrible time getting past this bump.  It was a hassle to close the curtain on account of this.

Not the case with these roll..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 30 errors, 23 borderline out of 80 examples."}
